english_name: The First Epistle General of John

verses.latin_vulgate: Videte qualem caritatem dedit nobis Pater, ut filii Dei nominemur et simus. Propter hoc mundus non novit nos: quia non novit eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἴδετε ποταπὴν ἀγάπην δέδωκεν ἡμῖν ὁ πατὴρ ἵνα τέκνα θεοῦ κληθῶμεν διὰ τοῦτο ὁ κόσμος οὐ γινώσκει ἡμᾶς ὅτι οὐκ ἔγνω αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Behold, what manner of love the Father hath bestowed upon us, that we should be called the sons of God: therefore the world knoweth us not, because it knew him not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Carissimi, nunc filii Dei sumus: et nondum apparuit quid erimus. Scimus quoniam cum apparuerit, similes ei erimus: quoniam videbimus eum sicuti est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀγαπητοὶ, νῦν τέκνα θεοῦ ἐσμεν καὶ οὔπω ἐφανερώθη τί ἐσόμεθα οἴδαμεν δὲ ὅτι ἐὰν φανερωθῇ ὅμοιοι αὐτῷ ἐσόμεθα ὅτι ὀψόμεθα αὐτὸν καθώς ἐστιν
verses.king_james: Beloved, now are we the sons of God, and it doth not yet appear what we shall be: but we know that, when he shall appear, we shall be like him; for we shall see him as he is.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et omnis qui habet hanc spem in eo, sanctificat se, sicut et ille sanctus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πᾶς ὁ ἔχων τὴν ἐλπίδα ταύτην ἐπ᾽ αὐτῷ ἁγνίζει ἑαυτὸν, καθὼς ἐκεῖνος ἁγνός ἐστιν
verses.king_james: And every man that hath this hope in him purifieth himself, even as he is pure.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis qui facit peccatum, et iniquitatem facit: et peccatum est iniquitas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶς ὁ ποιῶν τὴν ἁμαρτίαν, καὶ τὴν ἀνομίαν ποιεῖ καὶ ἡ ἁμαρτία ἐστὶν ἡ ἀνομία
verses.king_james: Whosoever committeth sin transgresseth also the law: for sin is the transgression of the law.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et scitis quia ille apparuit ut peccata nostra tolleret: et peccatum in eo non est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οἴδατε ὅτι ἐκεῖνος ἐφανερώθη ἵνα τὰς ἁμαρτίας ἡμῶν ἄρῃ καὶ ἁμαρτία ἐν αὐτῷ οὐκ ἔστιν
verses.king_james: And ye know that he was manifested to take away our sins; and in him is no sin.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis qui in eo manet, non peccat: et omnis qui peccat, non vidit eum, nec cognovit eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶς ὁ ἐν αὐτῷ μένων οὐχ ἁμαρτάνει πᾶς ὁ ἁμαρτάνων οὐχ ἑώρακεν αὐτὸν οὐδὲ ἔγνωκεν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Whosoever abideth in him sinneth not: whosoever sinneth hath not seen him, neither known him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Filioli, nemo vos seducat. Qui facit justitiam, justus est, sicut et ille justus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τεκνία, μηδεὶς πλανάτω ὑμᾶς ὁ ποιῶν τὴν δικαιοσύνην δίκαιός ἐστιν καθὼς ἐκεῖνος δίκαιός ἐστιν
verses.king_james: Little children, let no man deceive you: he that doeth righteousness is righteous, even as he is righteous.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui facit peccatum, ex diabolo est: quoniam ab initio diabolus peccat. In hoc apparuit Filius Dei, ut dissolvat opera diaboli.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ ποιῶν τὴν ἁμαρτίαν ἐκ τοῦ διαβόλου ἐστὶν ὅτι ἀπ᾽ ἀρχῆς ὁ διάβολος ἁμαρτάνει εἰς τοῦτο ἐφανερώθη ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ θεοῦ ἵνα λύσῃ τὰ ἔργα τοῦ διαβόλου
verses.king_james: He that committeth sin is of the devil; for the devil sinneth from the beginning. For this purpose the Son of God was manifested, that he might destroy the works of the devil.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis qui natus est ex Deo, peccatum non facit: quoniam semen ipsius in eo manet, et non potest peccare, quoniam ex Deo natus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶς ὁ γεγεννημένος ἐκ τοῦ θεοῦ ἁμαρτίαν οὐ ποιεῖ ὅτι σπέρμα αὐτοῦ ἐν αὐτῷ μένει καὶ οὐ δύναται ἁμαρτάνειν ὅτι ἐκ τοῦ θεοῦ γεγέννηται
verses.king_james: Whosoever is born of God doth not commit sin; for his seed remaineth in him: and he cannot sin, because he is born of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: In hoc manifesti sunt filii Dei, et filii diaboli. Omnis qui non est justus, non est ex Deo, et qui non diligit fratrem suum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν τούτῳ φανερά ἐστιν τὰ τέκνα τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ τὰ τέκνα τοῦ διαβόλου πᾶς ὁ μὴ ποιῶν δικαιοσύνην οὐκ ἔστιν ἐκ τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ ὁ μὴ ἀγαπῶν τὸν ἀδελφὸν αὑτοῦ
verses.king_james: In this the children of God are manifest, and the children of the devil: whosoever doeth not righteousness is not of God, neither he that loveth not his brother.

verses.latin_vulgate: quoniam hæc est annuntiatio, quam audistis ab initio, ut diligatis alterutrum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὄτι αὕτη ἐστὶν ἡ ἀγγελία ἣν ἠκούσατε ἀπ᾽ ἀρχῆς ἵνα ἀγαπῶμεν ἀλλήλους
verses.king_james: For this is the message that ye heard from the beginning, that we should love one another.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non sicut Cain, qui ex maligno erat, et occidit fratrem suum. Et propter quid occidit eum? Quoniam opera ejus maligna erant: fratris autem ejus, justa.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ καθὼς κάϊν ἐκ τοῦ πονηροῦ ἦν καὶ ἔσφαξεν τὸν ἀδελφὸν αὑτοῦ καὶ χάριν τίνος ἔσφαξεν αὐτόν ὅτι τὰ ἔργα αὐτοῦ πονηρὰ ἦν τὰ δὲ τοῦ ἀδελφοῦ αὐτοῦ δίκαια
verses.king_james: Not as Cain, *who* was of that wicked one, and slew his brother. And wherefore slew he him? Because his own works were evil, and his brother's righteous.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nolite mirari, fratres, si odit vos mundus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ θαυμάζετε ἀδελφοί μου εἰ μισεῖ ὑμᾶς ὁ κόσμος
verses.king_james: Marvel not, my brethren, if the world hate you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nos scimus quoniam translati sumus de morte ad vitam, quoniam diligimus fratres. Qui non diligit, manet in morte:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡμεῖς οἴδαμεν ὅτι μεταβεβήκαμεν ἐκ τοῦ θανάτου εἰς τὴν ζωὴν ὅτι ἀγαπῶμεν τοὺς ἀδελφούς ὁ μὴ ἀγαπῶν τὸν ἀδελφὸν μένει ἐν τῷ θανάτῳ
verses.king_james: We know that we have passed from death unto life, because we love the brethren. He that loveth not *his* brother abideth in death.

verses.latin_vulgate: omnis qui odit fratrem suum, homicida est. Et scitis quoniam omnis homicida non habet vitam æternam in semetipso manentem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶς ὁ μισῶν τὸν ἀδελφὸν αὑτοῦ ἀνθρωποκτόνος ἐστίν καὶ οἴδατε ὅτι πᾶς ἀνθρωποκτόνος οὐκ ἔχει ζωὴν αἰώνιον ἐν αὐτῷ μένουσαν
verses.king_james: Whosoever hateth his brother is a murderer: and ye know that no murderer hath eternal life abiding in him.

verses.latin_vulgate: In hoc cognovimus caritatem Dei, quoniam ille animam suam pro nobis posuit: et nos debemus pro fratribus animas ponere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν τούτῳ ἐγνώκαμεν τὴν ἀγάπην ὅτι ἐκεῖνος ὑπὲρ ἡμῶν τὴν ψυχὴν αὑτοῦ ἔθηκεν καὶ ἡμεῖς ὀφείλομεν ὑπὲρ τῶν ἀδελφῶν τὰς ψυχὰς τιθέναι
verses.king_james: Hereby perceive we the love *of God*, because he laid down his life for us: and we ought to lay down *our* lives for the brethren.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui habuerit substantiam hujus mundi, et viderit fratrem suum necessitatem habere, et clauserit viscera sua ab eo: quomodo caritas Dei manet in eo?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅς δ᾽ ἂν ἔχῃ τὸν βίον τοῦ κόσμου καὶ θεωρῇ τὸν ἀδελφὸν αὑτοῦ χρείαν ἔχοντα καὶ κλείσῃ τὰ σπλάγχνα αὑτοῦ ἀπ᾽ αὐτοῦ, πῶς ἡ ἀγάπη τοῦ θεοῦ μένει ἐν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: But whoso hath this world's good, and seeth his brother have need, and shutteth up his bowels *of compassion* from him, how dwelleth the love of God in him?

verses.latin_vulgate: Filioli mei, non diligamus verbo neque lingua, sed opere et veritate:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τεκνία μου μὴ ἀγαπῶμεν λόγῳ, μηδὲ γλώσσῃ ἀλλ᾽ ἔργῳ καὶ ἀληθείᾳ
verses.king_james: My little children, let us not love in word, neither in tongue; but in deed and in truth.

verses.latin_vulgate: in hoc cognoscimus quoniam ex veritate sumus: et in conspectu ejus suadebimus corda nostra.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐν τούτῳ γινώσκομεν ὅτι ἐκ τῆς ἀληθείας ἐσμὲν καὶ ἔμπροσθεν αὐτοῦ πείσομεν τὰς καρδίας ἡμῶν
verses.king_james: And hereby we know that we are of the truth, and shall assure our hearts before him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quoniam si reprehenderit nos cor nostrum: major est Deus corde nostro, et novit omnia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι ἐὰν καταγινώσκῃ ἡμῶν ἡ καρδία ὅτι μείζων ἐστὶν ὁ θεὸς τῆς καρδίας ἡμῶν καὶ γινώσκει πάντα
verses.king_james: For if our heart condemn us, God is greater than our heart, and knoweth all things.

verses.latin_vulgate: Carissimi, si cor nostrum non reprehenderit nos, fiduciam habemus ad Deum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀγαπητοὶ ἐὰν ἡ καρδία ἡμῶν μὴ καταγινώσκῃ ἡμῶν παῤῥησίαν ἔχομεν πρὸς τὸν θεόν
verses.king_james: Beloved, if our heart condemn us not, *then* have we confidence toward God.

verses.latin_vulgate: et quidquid petierimus, accipiemus ab eo: quoniam mandata ejus custodimus, et ea, quæ sunt placita coram eo, facimus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὃ ἐὰν αἰτῶμεν λαμβάνομεν παρ᾽ αὐτοῦ ὅτι τὰς ἐντολὰς αὐτοῦ τηροῦμεν καὶ τὰ ἀρεστὰ ἐνώπιον αὐτοῦ ποιοῦμεν
verses.king_james: And whatsoever we ask, we receive of him, because we keep his commandments, and do those things that are pleasing in his sight.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et hoc est mandatum ejus: ut credamus in nomine Filii ejus Jesu Christi: et diligamus alterutrum, sicut dedit mandatum nobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ αὕτη ἐστὶν ἡ ἐντολὴ αὐτοῦ ἵνα πιστεύσωμεν τῷ ὀνόματι τοῦ υἱοῦ αὐτοῦ ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ, καὶ ἀγαπῶμεν ἀλλήλους καθὼς ἔδωκεν ἐντολὴν ἡμῖν
verses.king_james: And this is his commandment, That we should believe on the name of his Son Jesus Christ, and love one another, as he gave us commandment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et qui servat mandata ejus, in illo manet, et ipse in eo: et in hoc scimus quoniam manet in nobis, de Spiritu quem dedit nobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὁ τηρῶν τὰς ἐντολὰς αὐτοῦ ἐν αὐτῷ μένει καὶ αὐτὸς ἐν αὐτῷ καὶ ἐν τούτῳ γινώσκομεν ὅτι μένει ἐν ἡμῖν ἐκ τοῦ πνεύματος οὗ ἡμῖν ἔδωκεν
verses.king_james: And he that keepeth his commandments dwelleth in him, and he in him. And hereby we know that he abideth in us, by the Spirit which he hath given us.
